text: Artificial intelligence is increasingly common in the modern workplace. One major impact is automation, where AI handles repetitive tasks like data entry or scheduling, freeing up employees for more creative work. Productivity tools, such as smart email assistants or project management software, help teams work faster and reduce errors. However, rather than replacing all jobs, many experts emphasize human-AI collaboration. For example, a doctor might use AI to analyze medical scans but still makes the final diagnosis. This means many job roles will change, requiring workers to learn new skills like interpreting AI data or managing automated systems.
